diffCoverage.notes: Traced credential handling end to end across the trust boundary: UI -> command/login (validate) -> zalo/login_profile (zca-rust) -> AccountProfile back to UI.

findings.evidence: Bearer tokens (imei/cookie/userAgent) enter only via the payload, are mapped to zca-rust inside the zalo layer, and are never logged. The command returns AccountProfile (id + optional name) only; the live smoke output is limited to uid length + has_display_name, confirmed by a secret-leak scan of the attested stdout sidecar.
findings.fix: When secure-cred-store lands, store these via the OS keychain only and keep them out of logs, app-data files, and IPC responses.

findings.evidence: External input (the JSON payload) is parsed and validated at the command boundary (golden principle #2) before any network use; malformed JSON / missing fields are rejected with value-free errors.
findings.fix: Keep validating at the command boundary as new account commands are added.

summary: Security review of the live-login path: credentials are validated at the command boundary and confined to the zalo layer; no token values are logged or returned across IPC, and the attested smoke sidecar was scanned and contains no secrets. Session handle is not yet persisted (Phase 3). No blocking findings.
